with real directories containing Copilot-specific files.

Previously the single commands/feature.md file caused Copilot CLI to
expose the command as /feature:feature. Now each sub-command is its
own file, yielding /feature:start, /feature:end, /feature:checkpoint,
/feature:status, /feature:catchup, /feature:handoff, /feature:resume.

- ${COPILOT_PLUGIN_ROOT} is used instead of ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}

  and /feature:status (not the old sub-command form)

commit.message:
P14: Fix spread-ignored ML label calculation in TradeFeatureExtractor

TradeFeatureExtractor.ExtractFeaturesWithOutcome now accounts for spread cost
when calculating the WasProfitable label. Previously, any price movement in the
correct direction was labeled as profitable, even if the spread would have
consumed the entire gain. This caused the ML model to overfit on marginal
trades that would actually be losing positions after transaction costs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(llm): MODEL_PROFILES per-model config, fix reasoning token budget

- Add MODEL_PROFILES dict with per-model: reasoning, supports_temperature,
  min_completion_tokens (16000 for reasoning, 4096 for standard),
  max_tokens_param, api type
- Add _get_profile() helper with prefix-matching for model variants
- Refactor _do_chat() and stream_chat() to use MODEL_PROFILES instead of
  hardcoded _reasoning_model checks (3 copy-pasted blocks → 1 source)
- Raise min budget from 8000 → 16000 for reasoning models (gpt-5-mini was
  using all 8000 on reasoning, leaving 0 for visible output → empty content
  falsely detected as [CONTENT_POLICY_BLOCKED])
- Add automatic retry with doubled budget when reasoning exhausts tokens
  (content=='' + finish_reason='length' + reasoning model)
- Remove llm_provider_override:minimax from bench YAMLs (brain, code-reviewer,
  secops-engineer) — use platform default GPT-5.x models
- Fix .env on node-2: removed wrong AZURE_DEPLOY_* overrides that mapped
  gpt-5-mini → gpt-4.1-mini (non-existent deployment)

Tested live: all 6 Azure models (gpt-5-mini/5/5.1/5.2/5.1-codex/4.1) produce
correct output with 16000 budget. Security analysis prompts that previously
returned [CONTENT_POLICY_BLOCKED] now produce 10K+ char responses.

fix: _convert_concrete_function switches initializer order for multi-layer Keras models (#799)

* Initial plan

* fix: use handle._unique_id matching instead of zip in _convert_concrete_function

`zip(cf.captured_inputs, cf.variables)` was unreliable for multi-layer
Keras models because:
1. cf.captured_inputs can include non-variable captures (training-phase
   flags etc.) that don't appear in cf.variables, causing zip to truncate
   or pair the wrong variable values with the wrong handles.
2. The ordering of the two sequences is not guaranteed to match.

Fix: build a {var.handle._unique_id -> variable} mapping from cf.variables,
then iterate cf.captured_inputs and look up each tensor's corresponding
variable by unique ID. Non-variable captures (no match) are silently skipped.

Also update tests: remove @unittest.skip from multi-layer tests and update
the regression test to expect success with a full round-trip check.

- On amd64 builds, force-reinstall CUDA 12.8 torch, torchaudio, and torchcodec versions to satisfy pyannote-audio dependency constraints

* chore: remove Red Hat UBI image support

Drop the UBI (Red Hat Universal Base Image) variant entirely.
UBI cannot install ffmpeg as a shared library, which is now
required by newer versions of whisperX. The project uses a
statically linked ffmpeg binary that is incompatible with this
requirement.
